notes: Models vibe's presession proc-<PID> → real session_<ts> dir → /clear → session_<ts2> dir lifecycle. Birth templated from the codex sibling: session_birth matches the proc-<PID> presession ready (CommandPattern pre-session), pid_bind binds within 1s, v1_session_handoff matches the real session_<ts> dir in ready (new_session); all v1 phases pinned to v1_session_handoff. old_session_ended requires transcript_removed for THAT session (the same-PID cleanup, pid_manager.go:566-576, #169 path — NOT the presession row); v2_session_birth requires new_session:true (a session_<ts2> dir never seen before, minted by /clear's reset_session rotating session_dir). Emission ORDERING is provisional: clear_history persists the old dir first, then _reset_session mints the new dir whose messages.jsonl may not materialize until turn 2 saves — so max_delay bounds are generous. BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false).

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of launch
PID bound within 1s (DiscoverPID by cwd + cmdline)
After the first prompt lands, the session_<ts> dir appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to the real vibe session
First user prompt drives ready → working on the session_<ts> session
First turn completes back to ready (same session dir)
Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — after /clear, a fresh session_<ts2> dir never seen before; the strongest observable proxy for 'content was reset'

Spec: 'Old session disappears' — same-PID cleanup emits transcript_removed for the FIRST session dir when the new session binds the same vibe PID. Bounds the visible overlap window (bound provisional pending record).
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on the first dir)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
